verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then delivered he Him therefore unto them to be crucified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they took Jesus, and led Him away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He, bearing His Cross, went forth into a place called the place of a skull, where they crucified Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Successes have terrible power to cast down or draw aside those who take not heed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus the Jews, who at first enjoyed the influence of God, sought the law of royalty from the Gentiles, and in the wilderness after the manna remembered the onions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the same way here, refusing the Kingdom of Christ, they invited to themselves that of Cæsar.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore God set a king over them, according to their own decision.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then Pilate heard these things, he delivered Him to be crucified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Utterly without reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when he ought to have enquired whether Christ had aimed at sovereign power, he pronounced the sentence through fear alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet that this might not befall him, Christ said beforehand, “My kingdom is not of this world”; but he having given himself wholly up to present things, would practice no great amount of wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet his wife’s dream should have been sufficient to terrify him; but by none of these things was he made better, nor did he look to heaven, but delivered Him up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And now they laid the cross upon Him as a malefactor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even the wood they abominated, and endured not even to touch it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This was also the case in the type; for Isaac bare the wood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But then the matter stopped at the will of his father, for it was the type; while here it proceeded to action, for it was the reality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And He came to the place of a skull.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some say that Adam died there, and there lieth; and that Jesus in this place where death had reigned, there also set up the trophy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He went forth bearing the Cross as a trophy over the tyranny of death: and as conquerors do, so He bare upon His shoulders the symbol of victory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What matter if the Jews did these things with a different intent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They crucified Him too with thieves, in this also unintentionally fulfilling prophecy; for what they did for insult contributed to the truth, that thou mayest learn how great is its power, since the Prophet had foretold of old, that “He was numbered with the transgressors.” ( Isa. liii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The devil therefore wished to cast a veil over what was done, but was unable; for the three were crucified, but Jesus alone was glorious, that thou mayest learn, that His power effected all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the miracles took place when the three had been nailed to the cross; but no one attributed anything of what was done to either of those others, but to Jesus only; so entirely was the plot of the devil rendered vain, and all returned upon his own head.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even of these two, one was saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He therefore did not insult the glory of the Cross, but contributed to it not a little.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was not a less matter than shaking the rocks, to change a thief upon the cross, and to bring him unto Paradise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And Pilate wrote a title.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the same time requiting the Jews, and making a defense for Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they had given Him up as worthless, and attempted to confirm this sentence by making Him share the punishment of the robbers, in order that for the future it might be in no man’s power to prefer evil charges against him, or to accuse him as a worthless and wicked person, to close moreover their mouths and the mouths of all who might desire to accuse Him, and to show that they had risen up against their own King, Pilate thus placed, as on a trophy, those letters, which utter a clear voice, and show forth His Victory, and proclaim His Kingdom, though not in its completeness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he made manifest not in a single tongue, but in three languages; for since it was likely that there would be a mixed multitude among the Jews on account of the Feast, in order that none might be ignorant of the defense, he publicly recorded the madness of the Jews, in all the languages.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they bore malice against Him even when crucified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet what did this harm you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if He was a mortal and weak, and was about to become extinct, why did ye fear the letters asserting that He is the King of the Jews?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what do they ask?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Say that ‘he said.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For now it is an assertion, and a general sentence, but if ‘he said’ be added, the charge is shown to be one arising from his own rashness and arrogance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still Pilate was not turned aside, but stood to his first decision.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it is no little thing that is dispensed even from this circumstance, but the whole matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since the wood of the cross was buried, because no one was careful to take it up, inasmuch as fear was pressing, and the believers were hurrying to other urgent matters; and since it was in after times to be sought for, and it was likely that the three crosses would lie together, in order that the Lord’s might not be unknown, it was made manifest to all, first by its lying in the middle, and then by the title.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For those of the thieves had no titles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The soldiers parted the garments, but not the coat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See the prophecies in every instance fulfilled by their wickednesses; for this also had been predicted of old; yet there were three crucified, but the matters of the prophecies were fulfilled in Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For why did they not this in the case of the others, but in His case only?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider too, I pray you, the exactness of the prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Prophet saith not only, that they “parted,” but that they “did not part.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The rest therefore they divided, the coat they divided not, but committed the matter to a decision by lot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the, “Woven from the top” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23 ) is not put without a purpose; but some say that a figurative assertion is declared by it, that the Crucified was not simply man, but had also the Divinity from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Others say that the Evangelist describes the very form of the coat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since in Palestine they put together two strips of cloth and so weave their garments, John, to show that the coat was of this kind, saith, “Woven from the top”; and to me he seems to say this, alluding to the poorness of the garments, and that as in all other things, so in dress also, He followed a simple fashion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things the soldiers did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He on the Cross, committeth His mother to the disciple, teaching us even to our last breath to show every care for our parents.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When indeed she unseasonably troubled Him, He said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?” ( c. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, “Who is My mother?” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here He showeth much loving affection, and committeth her to the disciple whom He loved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again John conceals himself, in modesty; for had he desired to boast, he would have also put in the cause for which he was loved, since probably it was some great and wonderful one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore doth He converse on nothing else with John, nor comfort him when desponding?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it was no time for comforting by words; besides, it was no little thing for him to be honored with such honor, and to receive the reward of steadfastness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But do thou consider, I pray, how even on the cross He did everything without being troubled, speaking with the disciple concerning His mother, fulfilling prophecies, holding forth good hopes to the thief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet before He was crucified He appeareth sweating, agonized, fearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then can this mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nothing difficult, nothing doubtful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There indeed the weakness of nature had been shown, here was being shown the excess of Power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, by these two things He teacheth us, even if before things terrible we be troubled, not on that account to shrink from things terrible, but when we have embarked in the contest to deem all things possible and easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then not tremble at death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Our soul hath by nature the love of life, but it lies with us either to loose the bands of nature, and make this desire weak; or else to tighten them, and make the desire more tyrannous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as we have the desire of sexual intercourse, but when we practice true wisdom we render the desire weak, so also it falls out in the case of life; and as God hath annexed carnal desire to the generation of children, to maintain a succession among us, without however forbidding us from traveling the higher road of continence; so also He hath implanted in us the love of life, forbidding us from destroying ourselves, but not hindering our despising the present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it behooves us, knowing this, to observe due measure, and neither to go at any time to death of our own accord, even though ten thousand terrible things possess us; nor yet when dragged to it, for the sake of what is pleasing to God, to shrink back from and fear it, but boldly to strip for it, preferring the future to the present life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the women stood by the Cross, and the weaker sex then appeared the manlier ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 25 ); so entirely henceforth were all things transformed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He, having committed His mother to John, said, “Behold thy Son.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O the honor! with what honor did He honor the disciple! when He Himself was now departing, He committed her to the disciple to take care of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since it was likely that, being His mother, she would grieve, and require protection, He with reason entrusted her to the beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To him He saith, “Behold thy mother.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He said, knitting them together in charity; which the disciple understanding, took her to his own home.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But why made He no mention of any other woman, although another stood there?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To teach us to pay more than ordinary respect to our mothers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as when parents oppose us on spiritual matters, we must not even own them, so when they do not hinder us, we ought to pay them all becoming respect, and to prefer them before others, because they begat us, because they bred us up, because they bare for us ten thousand terrible things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by these words He silenceth the shamelessness of Marcion; for if He were not born according to the flesh, nor had a mother, wherefore taketh He such forethought for her alone?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “that nothing was wanting to the Dispensation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He was everywhere desirous to show, that this Death was of a new kind, if indeed the whole lay in the power of the Person dying, and death came not on the Body before He willed it; and He willed it after He had fulfilled all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore also He said, “I have power to lay down My life; and I have power to take it again.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing therefore that all things were fulfilled, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again fulfilling a prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But consider, I pray, the accursed nature of the bystanders.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Though we have ten thousand enemies, and have suffered intolerable things at their hands, yet when we see them perishing, we relent; but they did not even so make peace with Him, nor were tamed by what they saw, but rather became more savage, and increased their irony; and having brought to Him vinegar on a sponge, as men bring it to the condemned, thus they gave Him to drink; since it is on this account that the hyssop is added.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Having therefore received it, He saith, It is finished.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He doth all things calmly, and with power?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what follows shows this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when all had been completed,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He bowed His head, (this had not been nailed,) and gave up the ghost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “died.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet to expire does not come after the bowing the head; but here, on the contrary, it doth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He did not, when He had expired, bow His head, as happens with us, but when He had bent His head, then He expired.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By all which things the Evangelist hath shown, that He was Lord of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the Jews, on the other hand, who swallowed the camel and strained at the gnat, having wrought so atrocious a deed, are very precise concerning the day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because it was the Preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross —they besought Pilate that their legs might be broken.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how strong a thing is truth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By means of the very things which are the objects of their zeal, prophecy is fulfilled, for by occasion of those things, this plain prediction, unconnected with them, receives its accomplishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the soldiers when they came, brake the legs of the others, but not those of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet these to gratify the Jews pierced His side with a spear, and now insulted the dead body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: O abominable and accursed purpose!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet, beloved, be not thou confounded, be not thou desponding; for the things which these men did from a wicked will, fought on the side of the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since there was a prophecy, saying, (from this circumstance, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 37; Zech. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not this only, but the deed then dared was a demonstration of the faith, to those who should afterwards disbelieve; as to Thomas, and those like him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With this too an ineffable mystery was accomplished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “there came forth water and blood.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not without a purpose, or by chance, did those founts come forth, but because by means of these two together the Church consisteth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the initiated know it, being by water indeed regenerate, and nourished by the Blood and the Flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence the Mysteries take their beginning; that when thou approachest to that awful cup, thou mayest so approach, as drinking from the very side.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And he that saw it bare record, and his record is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “I heard it not from others, but was myself present and saw it, and the testimony is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As may be supposed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he relates an insult done; he relates not anything great and admirable, that thou shouldest suspect his narrative; but securing the mouths of heretics, and loudly proclaiming beforehand the Mysteries that should be, and beholding the treasure laid up in them, he is very exact concerning what took place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that prophecy also is fulfilled,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “A bone of Him shall not be broken.” ( Ex. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 46; Num. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For even if this was said with reference to the lamb of the Jews, still it was for the sake of the reality that the type preceded, and in Him the prophecy was more fully accomplished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account the Evangelist brought forward the Prophet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since by continually producing himself as witness he would have seemed unworthy of credit, he brings Moses to help him, and saith, that neither did this come to pass without a purpose, but was written before of old.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is the meaning of the words, “A bone of Him shall not be broken.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again he confirms the Prophet’s words by his own witness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “These things,” saith he, “I have told you, that ye might learn that great is the connection of the type with the reality.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou what pains he takes to make that believed which seemed to be matter of reproach, and bringing shame?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that the soldier should insult even the dead body, was far worse than being crucified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But still, even these things,” he saith, “I have told, and told with much earnestness, ‘that ye might believe.’ ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let none then be unbelieving, nor through shame injure our cause.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the things which appear to be most shameful, are the very venerable records of our good things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After this came Joseph of Arimathæa, being a disciple.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not one of the twelve, but perhaps one of the seventy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For now deeming that the anger of the Jews was quenched by the Cross, they approached without fear, and took charge of His funeral.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Joseph therefore came and asked the favor from Pilate, which he granted; why should he not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nicodemus also assists him, and furnishes a costly burial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they were still disposed to think of Him as a mere man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they brought those spices whose especial nature is to preserve the body for a long time, and not to allow it quickly to yield to corruption, which was an act of men imagining nothing great respecting Him; but anyhow, they exhibited very loving affection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how did no one of the twelve come, neither John, nor Peter, nor any other of the more distinguished disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor doth the writer conceal this point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any one say that it was from fear of the Jews, these men also were occupied by the same fear; for Joseph too was, it saith, “A secret (disciple) for fear of the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not one can say that Joseph acted thus because he greatly despised them, but though himself afraid, still he came.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But John who was present, and had seen Him expire, did nothing of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It seems to me that Joseph was a man of high rank, (as is clear from the funeral,) and known to Pilate, on which account also he obtained the favor; and then he buried Him, not as a criminal, but magnificently, after the Jewish fashion, as some great and admirable one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And because they were straitened by the time, (since the Death took place at the ninth hour, and it is probable, that what with going to Pilate and what with taking down the body, evening would come upon them when it was not lawful to work,) they laid Him in the tomb that was near.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And it is providentially ordered, that He should be placed in a new tomb, wherein no one had been placed before, that His Resurrection might not be deemed to be that of some other who lay there with Him; and that the disciples might be able easily to come and be spectators of what came to pass, because the place was near; and that not they alone should be witnesses of His burial, but His enemies also, for the placing seals on the tomb, and the sitting by of the soldiers to watch it, were the actions of men testifying to the burial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Christ earnestly desired that this should be confessed, no less than the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also the disciples are very earnest about this, the showing that He died.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Resurrection all succeeding time would confirm, but the Death, if at that time it had been partially concealed, or not made very manifest, was likely to harm the account of the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor was it for these reasons only that He was laid near, but also that the story about the stealing might be proved false.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The first day of the week” (that is, the Lord’s day) “cometh Mary Magdalene, very early in the morning, and seeth the stone taken away from the sepulcher.” ( Ch. xx. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He arose while both stone and seals lay over Him; but because it was necessary that others should be fully satisfied, the tomb was opened after the Resurrection, and thus what had come to pass was confirmed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This then was what moved Mary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For being entirely full of loving affection towards her Master, when the Sabbath was past, she could not bear to rest, but came very early in the morning, desiring to find some consolation from the place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when she saw the place, and the stone taken away, she neither entered in nor stooped down, but ran to the disciples, in the greatness of her longing; for this was what she earnestly desired, she wished very speedily to learn what had become of the body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This was the meaning of her running, and her words declare it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They have taken away,” she saith, “my Lord, and I know not where they have laid Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how she knew not as yet anything clearly concerning the Resurrection, but thought there had been a removal of the body, and tells all simply to the disciples?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Evangelist hath not deprived the woman of such a praise, nor thought it shame that they should have learnt these things first from her who had passed the night in watching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus everywhere doth the truth-loving nature of his disposition shine forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When then she came and said these things, they hearing them, draw near with great eagerness to the sepulcher, and see the linen clothes lying, which was a sign of the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither, if any persons had removed the body, would they before doing so have stripped it; nor if any had stolen it, would they have taken the trouble to remove the napkin, and roll it up, and lay it in a place by itself; but how? they would have taken the body as it was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account John tells us by anticipation that it was buried with much myrrh, which glues linen to the body not less firmly than lead; in order that when thou hearest that the napkins lay apart, thou mayest not endure those who say that He was stolen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a thief would not have been so foolish as to spend so much trouble on a superfluous matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For why should he undo the clothes? and how could he have escaped detection if he had done so? since he would probably have spent much time in so doing, and be found out by delaying and loitering.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why do the clothes lie apart, while the napkin was wrapped together by itself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest learn that it was not the action of men in confusion or haste, the placing some in one place, some in another, and the wrapping them together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this they believed in the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account Christ afterwards appeared to them, when they were convinced by what they had seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe too here again the absence of boastfulness in the Evangelist, how he witnesses to the exactness of Peter’s search.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he himself having gotten before Peter, and having seen the linen clothes, enquired not farther, but withdrew; but that fervent one passing farther in, looked at everything carefully, and saw somewhat more, and then the other too was summoned to the sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he entering after Peter, saw the grave-clothes lying, and separate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now to separate, and to place one thing by itself, and another, after rolling it up, by itself, was the act of some one doing things carefully, and not in a chance way, as if disturbed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But do thou, when thou hearest that thy Lord arose naked, cease from thy madness about funerals; for what is the meaning of that superfluous and unprofitable expense, which brings much loss to the mourners, and no gain to the departed, or (if we must say that it brings anything) rather harm?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the costliness of burial hath often caused the breaking open of tombs, and hath caused him to be cast out naked and unburied, who had been buried with much care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But alas for vainglory!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How great the tyranny which it exhibits even in sorrow! how great the folly!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many, that this may not happen, having cut in pieces those fine clothes, and filled them with many spices, so that they may be doubly useless to those who would insult the dead, then commit them to the earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Are not these the acts of madmen? of men beside themselves? to make a show of their ambition, and then to destroy it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yea,” saith some one, “it is in order that they may lie safely with the dead that we use all these contrivances.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Well then, if the robbers do not get them, will not the moths get them, and the worms?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if the moths and worms get them not, will not time and the moisture of putrefaction destroy them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us suppose that neither tomb-breakers, nor moths, nor worms, nor time, nor anything else, destroy what lies in the tomb, but that the body itself remains untouched until the Resurrection, and these things are preserved new and fresh and fine; what advantage is there from this to the departed, when the body is raised naked, while these remain here, and profit us nothing for those accounts which must be given?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wherefore then,” saith some one, “was it done in the case of Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First of all, do not compare these with human matters, since the harlot poured even ointment upon His holy feet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if we must speak on these things, we say, that they were done when the doers knew not the word of the Resurrection; therefore it saith, “As was the manner of the Jews.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who honored Christ were not of the twelve, but were those who did not honor Him greatly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The twelve honored Him not in this way, but by death and massacre and dangers for His sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That other indeed was honor, but far inferior to this of which I have spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, as I began by saying, we are now speaking of men, but at that time these things were done with relation to the Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn that Christ made no account of these things, He said, “Ye saw Me an hungered, and ye fed Me; thirsty, and ye gave Me drink; naked, and ye clothed Me” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 35 ); but nowhere did He say, “dead, and ye buried Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this I say not as taking away the custom of burial, (that be far from me,) but as cutting short its extravagance and unseasonable vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “feeling and grief and sympathy for the departed persuade to this practice.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The practice doth not proceed from sympathy for the departed, but from vainglory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since if thou desirest to sympathize with the dead, I will show thee another way of mourning, and will teach thee to put on him garments which shall rise again with him, and make him glorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these garments are not consumed by worms, nor wasted by time, nor stolen by tomb-breakers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of what sort then are these?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The clothing of alms-doing; for this is a robe that shall rise again with him, because the seal of alms-doing is with him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With these garments shine they who then hear, “Hungering ye fed Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These make men distinguished, these make them glorious, these place them in safety; but those used now are only something for moths to consume, and a table for worms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this I say, not forbid ding to use funeral observance, but bidding you to do it with moderation, so as to cover the body, and not commit it naked to the earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if living He biddeth us have no more than enough to cover us, much more when dead; since the dead body hath not so much need of garments as when it is living and breathing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when alive, on account of the cold, and for decency’s sake, we need the covering of garments, but when dead we require grave-clothes for none of these reasons, but that the body may not lie naked; and better than grave-clothes we have the earth, fairest of coverings, and more suited for the nature of such bodies as ours.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then where there are so many needs we must not search for anything superfluous, much more where there is no such necessity, is the ostentation unseasonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But the lookers-on will laugh,” saith some one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Most certainly if there be any laughter, we need not care much for one so exceedingly foolish; but at present there are many who rather admire and accept our true wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these are not the things which deserve laughter, but those which we do at present, weeping, and wailing, and burying ourselves with the departed; these things deserve ridicule and punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But to show true wisdom, both in these respects and in the modesty of the attire used, prepares crowns and praises for us, and all will applaud us, and will admire the power of Christ, and will say, “Amazing!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How great is the power of the Crucified One!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath persuaded those who are perishing and wasting, that death is not death; they therefore do not act as perishing men, but as men who send the dead before them to a distant and better dwelling-place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He hath persuaded them that this corruptible and earthy body shall put on a garment more glorious than silk or cloth of gold, the garment of immortality; therefore they are not very anxious about their burial, but deem a virtuous life to be an admirable winding-sheet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things they will say, if they see us showing true wisdom; but if they behold us bent down with grief, playing the woman, placing around troops of female mourners, they will laugh, and mock, and find fault in ten thousand ways, pulling to pieces our foolish expense, our vain labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With these things we hear all finding fault; and very reasonably.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what excuse can we have, when we adorn a body, which is consumed by corruption and worms, and neglect Christ when thirsting, going about naked, and a stranger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Cease we then from this vain trouble.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us perform the obsequies of the departed, as is good both for us and them, to the glory of God: let us do much alms for their sake, let us send with them the best provision for the way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if the memory of admirable men, though dead, hath protected the living, (for, “I will defend,” it saith, “this city for Mine Own. sake, and for My servant David’s sake”— 2 Kings xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 34 ,) much more will alms-doing effect this; for this hath raised even the dead, as when the widows stood round showing what things Dorcas had made, while she was with them. ( Acts ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore one is about to die, let the friend of that dying person prepare the obsequies, and persuade the departing one to leave somewhat to the needy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With these garments let him send him to the grave, leaving Christ his heir.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if they who write kings among their heirs, leave a safe portion to their relations, when one leaves Christ heir with his children, consider how great good he will draw down upon himself and all his.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These are the right sort of funerals, these profit both those who remain and those who depart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we be so buried, we shall be glorious at the Resurrection-time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if caring for the body we neglect the soul, we then shall suffer many terrible things, and incur much ridicule.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For neither is it a common unseemliness to depart without being clothed with virtue, nor is the body, though cast out without a tomb, so disgraced, as a soul appearing bare of virtue in that day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This let us put on, this let us wrap around us; it is best to do so during all our lifetime; but if we have in this life been negligent, let us at least in our end be sober, and charge our relations to help us when we depart by alms-doing; that being thus assisted by each other, we may attain to much confidence, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, dominion, and honor, now and ever and world without end.
